title: chore(enter-tierprog): remove dead flows code and fix stale README
author: voodoohop
pr_body_excerpt:
Dead-code cleanup in tier-progression maintenance scripts (no production path, no tests):

- Delete the never-called `ban_deleted_accounts` function (d1_updates.py)
- Remove the dead `split` / `retry` CLI subcommands (cleanup-github-users.ts) — only `audit`/`apply` are used
- Collapse the speculative, never-configured GitHub-App JWT auth path down to a single `GITHUB_TOKEN` PAT (kept the `TokenProvider` interface so call sites/log lines are unchanged)
- Fix the stale README: drop 3 referenced files that don't exist, correct the spore-to-microbe pipeline (scan → enrich → review → apply), remove the false workflow trigger

Net −251 lines. biome clean; cleanup-github-users.ts type-checks; no test references any deleted symbol. Skipped the queryD1/CSV dedups (held for separate review).

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

gist.headline: Spring cleaning the hive
gist.blurb: We did some spring cleaning in our tier-progression scripts, sweeping away dead code and updating the README. A tidier codebase means a happier hive!
gist.summary: Removed 251 lines of dead code from the tier-progression maintenance scripts, including unused CLI subcommands and legacy GitHub-App JWT auth paths, and updated the README to reflect the current pipeline.
gist.impact: Internal maintenance scripts are now cleaner and easier to maintain, reducing technical debt without affecting production workflows.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee sweeping up or pruning dead pixelated leaves/code blocks in a cozy garden shed. Nomnom creature eating the discarded code.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of Polly the bee mascot wearing a tiny gardener's apron, sweeping up dead pixelated leaves and old code blocks in a warm, glowing garden shed. The round tan Nomnom creature happily eats the discarded code from a dustpan. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and warm peach gradient background. Chunky retro sprites, warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley.
